div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 17px; margin-left: 2px;

div.div: Batocera Swarm — enable_edge = TRUE (Edge deployed)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1670px; height: 1px; padding-top: 55px; margin-left: 10px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Every Drone holds ONE persistent OUTBOUND mux to the always-on Edge — no inbound ports, no port-forward, no public IP. The control plane only authorizes transfers (mints a short-lived token). ROM bytes move Drone↔Drone over the best tier with fall-through: LAN-direct → direct-public → UDP hole-punch → Edge RELAY (last resort). The Edge relays opaque DATA only when direct fails, and never carries bytes on the other tiers or through the control plane. reachability probe AUTO-OFF.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 668px; height: 1px; padding-top: 145px; margin-left: 32px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Drone A — RECEIVER
persistent outbound mux → Edge
TransportSelector.fetch:
1) LAN-direct
2) direct-public
3) hole-punch (UDP + reliable-ARQ)
4) Edge relay
one active dl · progress · cancel
resume · hash verify

TIER 1 — LAN-direct ✓
mTLS /peer/* to B.local_ip (same LAN) — Edge NOT used for bytes

div.div:
TIER 3 — hole-punched UDP (reliable-ARQ)
DIRECT Drone↔Drone · bytes do NOT touch the Edge

div.div:

(RELAY_OPEN/DATA over mux)

div.div:
relay leg
(last resort)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 630px; height: 1px; padding-top: 593px; margin-left: 670px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
LEGEND
── orange = persistent outbound mux / Edge relay leg
── blue = control-plane HTTPS (authorize / mint token)
── green (thick) = direct P2P data path (LAN or hole-punch) — Edge carries NO bytes
── purple dashed = lean DB writes (presence · transfer_sessions)
Tiers are tried best-first: LAN → direct-public → hole-punch → relay (fall-through on failure).
